Article Title: 'No faction of Sena other than Eknath Shinde led Shiv Sena remains': Amit Shah
Article Text:
Amit Shah made the remarks at a rally in Kolhapur (ANI)

PUNE: Taking a jibe at Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray over his party’s six MPs on the verge of joining Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena, Union Home Minister Amit Shah on Saturday said that other than Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena, there is no other Sena faction left now.Shah visited Maharashtra’s Kolhapur district to address a couple of rallies. The Union Home Minister arrived in the state against the backdrop of much-speculated ‘Operation Tiger’, where six rebel MPs of Uddhav’s party are expected to join Deputy Chief minister Eknath Shinde ’s faction of Shiv Sena in the coming days.During his public address, Shah referred to Shinde as the chief of Shiv Sena. “Earlier we were required to call it the Shinde faction (of Shiv Sena). However, now there are no other factions left,” Shah said.The Union Home Minister criticised Uddhav Thackeray over his alliance with Congress. He said, “Uddhav is sitting on the lap of Congress, which uses infiltrators as its vote bank. I want to make it clear that India is not a ‘Dharamshala’. All the infiltrators will be thrown out of the country.”Mentioning the victory in West Bengal Assembly polls, Shah said, “While BJP was winning in various states, people would ask us what would happen to our party in Bengal.However, the people of Bengal blessed us with a massive victory.”“BJP and Prime Minister Narendra Modi are indebted to the people of Bengal. I want to tell the people of Bengal that we will not only stop the infiltration from the border areas of Bengal, our govt will also ensure that every infiltrator of the country will be identified and thrown out of the country,” Home Minister asserted.Shah was in Kolhapur district of Maharashtra, where he laid a foundation stone for the Rs 1,500 crore project for conservation and development work of Mahalaxmi Temple on Saturday.
Article Summary: Shah visited Maharashtra’s Kolhapur district to address a couple of rallies. The Union Home Minister arrived in the state against the backdrop of much-speculated ‘Operation Tiger’ Six rebel MPs of Shiv Sena are expected to join Deputy Chief minister Eknath Shinde ’s faction in coming days.

Article Title: 'Will cooperate but others should be questioned too': Senior TMC MLA Sovandeb Chattopadhyay on signature forgery case probe
Article Text:
File photo: TMC leader Sovandeb Chattopadhyay (PTI Photo)

Poll Do you believe Sovandeb Chattopadhyay should have full cooperation from TMC legislators during the CID probe? Yes, he deserves full support. No, others should be investigated too.

NEW DELHI: Senior Trinamool Congress (TMC) MLA Sovandeb Chattopadhyay on Saturday assured full cooperation with the West Bengal CID's probe into the alleged signature forgery case, but maintained that others who were present at the time should also be questioned.His remarks came after he was questioned for over an hour by a two-member team of the state's Criminal Investigation Department at his residence in Kolkata's Bhawanipur, PTI reported.“I will cooperate with the probe more than 200%. But those who made the allegations of signature forgery should also be questioned, because everyone was present when the proposal was signed,” the Ballygunge MLA told reporters.The controversy revolves around a letter submitted to assembly Speaker Rathindra Bose by the Trinamool faction led by its supremo and former chief minister Mamata Banerjee, proposing Sovandeb Chattopadhyay as leader of the opposition. The letter reportedly carried the signature of TMC national general secretary Abhishek Banerjee , Mamata Banerjee's nephew.The Speaker, however, declined to accept the communication after allegations emerged that signatures on the document had been forged.The complaint was lodged by legislators Ritabrata Banerjee and Sandipan Saha, both of whom were later expelled from the party.Escalating the leadership tussle, Ritabrata subsequently submitted another letter claiming the support of 59 MLAs and was eventually recognised by the Speaker as leader of the opposition.The TMC secured 80 seats in the april Assembly elections, ending its 15-year tenure in power.The new BJP-led state government later handed the alleged forgery case to the Criminal Investigation Department for investigation.As part of the probe, CID officers have questioned Abhishek Banerjee multiple times and collected handwriting samples from several TMC legislators. While some MLAs have been served notices, others have been questioned at their residences.On Friday, CID officers visited the residence of senior TMC MP Kalyan Banerjee to serve a notice in connection with the case. The notice could not be delivered as he was in Delhi and his family members declined to accept it.The investigation has unfolded alongside a legal battle over the opposition leadership in the Assembly. Chattopadhyay moved the Calcutta High Court challenging the Speaker’s decision to recognise Ritabrata Banerjee as Leader of the Opposition, but a single bench declined to grant interim relief.He has since approached a division bench against the order, with the matter expected to be heard next week.
Article Summary: Sovandeb Chattopadhyay assured full cooperation with the West Bengal CID's probe into the alleged signature forgery case. His remarks came after he was questioned for over an hour by a two-member team of the state's Criminal Investigation Department at his residence in Kolkata's Bhawanipur.

Article Title: Union minister Bandi Sanjay's son gets bail in POCSO case
Article Text:
Bageerath with Union minister Bandi Sanjay

Poll Do you believe that interim bail should be granted in cases involving serious allegations like POCSO? Yes, it depends on the circumstances No, it should be denied in such cases

NEW DELHI: A Hyderabad court granted interim bail until June 25 to Bageerath, son of Union minister Bandi Sanjay Kumar, who was arrested in connection with a case registered under the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (POCSO) Act.Bageerath, who had been lodged in judicial custody, secured temporary relief from the court on Friday to enable him to appear for his BBA examinations.He was taken into custody on the night of May 16 following the registration of the case and was later remanded to judicial custody by a magistrate. Prior to his arrest, the Telangana High Court had declined to provide him interim protection from coercive action.The case was registered at the Petbasheerabad police station on May 8 under relevant provisions of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) and the POCSO Act based on a complaint filed by the mother of a 17-year-old girl.According to the complaint, Bageerath was in a relationship with the minor and had allegedly subjected her to sexual harassment. After investigators recorded the girl's statement, additional and more serious charges under the BNS and POCSO Act were incorporated into the case.Bageerath, however, has denied the allegations and filed a counter-complaint of his own. In his version, he alleged that the girl had introduced herself to him and had invited him to family events and social gatherings.He stated that, trusting the family, he accompanied them on group visits to certain religious places. He further claimed that the girl and her parents later pressured him to marry her and allegedly demanded money when he refused.According to his complaint, he was threatened with false cases if he did not comply with their demands. Police subsequently registered an FIR on the basis of his complaint as well.The matter remains under investigation.
Article Summary: Bageerath, son of Union minister Bandi Sanjay Kumar, granted interim bail until June 25. He was arrested in connection with a case registered under the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (POCSO) Act. The case was registered at the Petbasheerabad police station on May 8.

Article Title: 'Raises serious questions on security': Abhishek Banerjee after 'armed individuals' apprehended at Kolkata airport
Article Text:
The incident allegedly occured shortly after Abhishek Banerjee landed in Kolkata from Delhi

Also Read |

NEW DELHI: Trinamool Congress (TMC) national general secretary Abhishek Banerjee on Saturday raised concerns over law and order in West Bengal after an allegedly armed individual was detained at Kolkata Airport on Friday night.In a post on X, he said, “Last night at Kolkata Airport, shortly after I landed from Delhi, a shocking incident took place. Armed individuals were apprehended, leading to serious questions about security and the lengths to which some are willing to go,” and shared a video of a person holding what appeared to be a pistol.The Trinamool second-in-command had travelled to Delhi earlier in the day to meet Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla and to raise objections over the merger of rebel TMC MPs with the Tripura-based National Citizens’ Party of India (NCPI).Separately, TMC MP Derek O'Brien also alleged that a “BJP supporter” was seen at the airport exit with a firearm when he and Abhishek Banerjee were leaving the premises. He shared videos on X showing a person being escorted away and claimed it was an “attempt to murder” the Diamond Harbour MP.“BJP SUPPORTER WITH A GUN. Chilling video. More proof from outside Kolkata airport last night. Attempt to murder?” O'Brien posted.In another post, the Rajya Sabha member shared a video from the TMC’s official handle showing a person being surrounded and restrained, stating that the individual was caught by women supporters of the party. He also questioned the role of West Bengal Police in the incident.“Minutes before @abhishekaitc along with @KBanerjee_AITC and I walked out of Kolkata airport at around 9.45 pm. Armed man at exit. Nabbed by women supporters of TMC. Bengal Police? Videos say it all,” he wrote.The TMC alleged that the incident was a “state-sponsored attempt to eliminate a political opponent” and raised concerns about law and order in the state under the leadership of Suvendu Adhikari, West Bengal's new chief minister.The fresh incident comes amid rising political tensions in West Bengal. After TMC’s defeat in the April assembly elections, which ended party supremo Mamata Banerjee’s 15-year tenure as chief minister, several Trinamool leaders, including her nephew Abhishek Banerjee, have been attacked with eggs in separate incidents.
Article Summary: The incident allegedly occured shortly after Abhishek Banerjee landed in Kolkata from Delhi. The Trinamool second-in-command had travelled to Delhi earlier in the day to meet Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla. The fresh incident comes amid rising political tensions in West Bengal.

Article Title: Delhi plans 4am buses to improve women’s safety
Article Text:
**EDS: THIRD PARTY IMAGE** In this image posted on March 28, 2026, Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta, left, meets Delhi LG Taranjit Singh Sandhu, at Lok Niwas in New Delhi. (@LokNiwasDelhi/X via PTI Photo)(PTI03_28_2026_000067B)

New Delhi:





In a bid to improve safety for women commuters in the early hours, the govt is planning to introduce Delhi Transport Corporation (DTC) bus services from 4 am.Sharing the proposal, lieutenant governor TS Sandhu said several measures are being considered to enhance safety across the city. “As part of these efforts, we are working on a plan to launch early-morning bus services for women commuters, beginning at 4 am, with buses operating at one-hour intervals. Initially, these buses will be driven by women drivers and may also have women police personnel and marshals on board,” he said.Delhi govt already offers free travel to women and transgender residents on DTC and cluster buses through the Pink Saheli Smart Card, which replaced the earlier paper-based ticket system.Sandhu also stressed strengthening the LG Listening Posts initiative. “When we have portals in place, efforts should be made to improve monitoring and operations. I have asked the commissioner to encourage people to upload photographs of unauthorised construction so that genuine cases can be prioritised,” he said.Referring to recent building collapses and fire incidents, the LG underlined accountability. “Responsibility must be fixed. Authorities cannot evade accountability by claiming that an issue falls outside their jurisdiction,” he said.
Article Summary: Delhi govt plans to introduce Delhi Transport Corporation (DTC) bus services from 4 am. The buses will be driven by women drivers and may also have women police personnel and marshals on board. Delhi govt already offers free travel to women and transgender residents on DTC and cluster buses.

Article Title: Delhi Police arrests man who posed as ADM to swindle Rs 60 lakh
Article Text: Delhi Police arrests man who posed as ADM to swindle Rs 60 lakh
Article Summary: Delhi Police arrests man who posed as ADM to swindle Rs 60 lakh. Police: He posed as an ADM and tried to convince people to hand over cash. He was arrested after allegedly swindling a man of Rs60 lakh from a bank.

Article Title: ​War of words between Ashtikar’s son & Andhare
Article Text:
Krushna Patil

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar:





A public spat has broken out between Krushna Patil, the son of rebel Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Nagesh Patil Ashitikar, and Sushama Andhare, the deputy leader of the Uddhav Thackeray faction of Shiv Sena, amid tensions over alleged defections under ‘Operation Tiger’.The row escalated after Krushna Patil, who is the MVA-backed candidate for the Nanded MLC poll, distanced himself from his father, who is among the MPs allegedly being targeted for poaching, while reiterating his loyalty to Uddhav Thackeray. “I am not in touch with my father,” Patil said, even as he claimed ignorance about Ashitikar’s future political move.Reacting sharply, Andhare questioned his stance and demanded that he perform the symbolic last rites of his father to prove loyalty. “Why is he trying to be smart? He is like his father only,” she said in Mumbai on Friday.Hitting back, Patil termed Andhare “mentally imbalanced”. “If I rake up Andhare’s past, she would be in big trouble. I request her not to stoop too low. Otherwise, I too can retaliate,” he told reporters in Nanded on Saturday.He also criticised Andhare for targeting him during the MLC election. “While the local Congress unit has issued a whip to support me, Andhare and others were speaking against me,” he said.Drawing parallels within Maharashtra politics, Patil cited the example of Gajanan Kirtikar and his son Amol Kirtikar, who ended up on opposite sides after the Shiv Sena split.Patil contested the Nanded local authorities’ constituency poll against Amar Rajurkar of BJP as the MVA-sponsored candidate.
Article Summary: Krushna Patil is the son of rebel Shiv Sena MP Nagesh Patil Ashitikar. His father is among the MPs allegedly being targeted for poaching. Patil has distanced himself from his father, while reiterating his loyalty to Uddhav Thackeray.

Article Title: Kolhapur police tell devotees not to visit Mahalaxmi temple between 10.00 am to 11.00 am to avoid likely inconvenience due to Amit Shah’s visit
Article Text:
Kolhapur





: Police have appealed to devotees not to visit Mahalaxmi temple between 10 am and 11 am on Saturday to avoid inconvenience they might face due to the visit of union home minister Amit Shah The Kolhapur police issued a statement stating that the temple will not shut for the darshan. “During the visit of Shah, the temple will not shut for darshan, however to ensure safety arrangements, the devotees should visit the temple at other times,,” the statement released by the Kolhapur Police.Shah along with chief minister Devendra Fadnavis , deputy CM’s Eknath Shinde and Sunetra Pawar and several other state ministers from Maharashtra govt are going to attend the event of foundation laying of new Pradakshina Marg, and conservation of an old temple located in the premise of Mahalaxmi temple.Chandrakant Patil, senior BJP leader and state higher education minister said, “The Union home minister is a devotee of goddess Mahalaxmi. He has approved Rs 1500 cr from the Centre out of which Rs 900 crore will be spent on land acquisition and Rs 600 cr on beautification works. He wished to be at the temple on the first day of start of the works and accordingly he is arriving. We are also preparing for other works at the temple for which we are confident that union home minister will help get us funds. After the event, he will address rally at Kolhapur ‘Dhanyawad Amitbhai’ for sanctioning funds for Mahalaxmi temple and then at Ichalkaranji he will address rally on completion of 12 years of Prime Minister Narendra Modi .”
Article Summary: Kolhapur police issued a statement stating that the temple will not shut for the darshan. Amit Shah along with chief minister Devendra Fadnavis, deputy CM’s Eknath Shinde and Sunetra Pawar and several other state ministers from Maharashtra govt will attend the event.

Article Title: Teen who rammed SUV into woman before crashing into 6 vehicles in Navi Mumbai booked for drunk driving
Article Text:
Drunk teen SUV driver drags woman 50m, hits 6 parked vehicles

Watch CCTV Captures Delhi Teen’s Speeding SUV Seconds Before Fatal Dwarka Crash CCTV Captures Delhi Teen’s Speeding SUV Seconds Before Fatal Dwarka Crash

NAVI MUMBAI: The CBD-Belapur police have invoked section 185 of the Motor Vehicle Act for drunk driving against the accused 19-year-old SUV driver.The teenager had hit and dragged a woman pedestrian for 50 metres and caused her severe injuries and then rammed into six vehicles parked on the roadside in Artist village, sector-8 on Friday.Arun Pawar, senior inspector of CBD-Belapur said, the accused SUV driver Vikram Kamble's medical test report received from NMMC hospital, Nerul confirmed that he was under the influence of alcohol while driving.Subsequently, relevant section 185 Of Motor Vehicle Act that penalizes drunk driving were invoked against him and will be mentioned in the chargesheet that will be submitted in the court.The woman Mohini Shinde (35), who worked as househelp, sustained severe head injuries and bruises on her face and near the knees is recuperating at MGM hospital, CBD-Belapur and is out of danger, informed doctors.The accident took place in the residential locality of Artist village in Sector-8, CBD-Belapur around 5.30 pm on Friday. Police said the speeding SUV hit the woman from behind while she was walking close to the pavement and dragged her for nearly 50 metres before crashing into six vehicles parked along the roadside.Local residents rushed the injured woman to MGM Hospital, where she received prompt treatment. The incident was also captured on CCTV cameras installed in the area.Police said the SUV driver Vikram Gangadhar Kamble (19) was accompanied by his relative Akash Chandrakant Kamble (26), a private chauffeur. Both were detained by locals at the spot before the police arrived, following which an FIR was registered for rash and negligent driving and damage to property.
Article Summary: The CBD-Belapur police have invoked section 185 of the Motor Vehicle Act for drunk driving against the accused 19-year-old SUV driver. The teenager had hit and dragged a woman pedestrian for 50 metres and caused her severe injuries and then rammed into six vehicles parked on the roadside. The woman Mohini Shinde (35), who worked as househelp, sustained severe head injuries and bruises on her face and near the knees.

Article Title: No Sena faction exists other than Shinde-led Shiv Sena: Shah
Article Text:
Union home minister Amit Shah receives a memento during the 'Dhanyawad Sabha' in Kolhapur on Saturday in the presence of Maharashtra chief minister Devendra Fadnavis (extreme left) and deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde (extreme right)

Pune:





Union home minister Amit Shah on Saturday said there was no Sena faction now other than Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena, taking a jibe at Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray over his party’s six MPs on the verge of joining the Shinde group.Shah arrived in Kolhapur district of Maharashtra in the early hours of Saturday against the backdrop of much-speculated “Operation Tiger”. Six rebel MPs of Uddhav’s party are expected to join deputy chief minister Shinde’s Shiv Sena in the coming days.The Union home minister referred to Shinde as the chief of Shiv Sena during his public address in Kolhapur city. “Earlier, we had to call it the Shinde faction (of Shiv Sena). Now, no other faction is left,” he said.Shah criticised Thackeray over his alliance with Congress and said, “Uddhav is sitting on the lap of Congress, which uses infiltrators as its vote bank. I want to make it clear that India is not a ‘Dharamshala’. All infiltrators will be thrown out of the country.”Referring to the victory in West Bengal Assembly polls, Shah said, “While BJP was winning in various states, people would ask us what would happen to our party in Bengal.The people of Bengal blessed us with a massive victory.”Shah said, “BJP and Prime Minister Narendra Modi are indebted to the people of Bengal. I want to tell the people of Bengal that we will not only stop infiltration through the state’s border areas, our govt will also ensure that every infiltrator is identified and thrown out of the country.”In another rally in Kolhapur’s Ichalkaranji, Shah attacked Congress and said the party did very little for farmers when it was in the govt. He said, “Congress was in the govt for so many years but it never thought of farmers. Rahul Gandhi claims that his party waived off farm loan, but he should give details of how much was the waiver.”Shah said, “In the 10 years of their (UPA) govt, they waived off only Rs60,000 crore farm loan. Under Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s leadership, our govt in the past 12 years has directly deposited Rs4,28,000 crore into the accounts of farmers irrespective of their loan status. Therefore, I appeal to people to vote for a govt that will think about common people and work tirelessly for development.”Comparing Modi’s 12 years with that of the previous govt, Shah said, “India has witnessed terror attacks and blasts before Prime Minister Modi took over 12 years ago. The Prime Minister then would be silent despite attacks on India. Prime Minister Modi changed that response through surgical strikes, aerial strikes and Operation Sindoor.”Shah also laid the foundation stone of a Rs1,500crore project for conservation and development of Mahalaxmi Temple.
Article Summary: Shah arrived in Kolhapur district of Maharashtra in the early hours of Saturday against the backdrop of much-speculated ‘Operation Tiger’ Six rebel MPs of Uddhav’s party are expected to join deputy chief minister Shinde's Shiv Sena.

Article Title: New police commissioners take charge in Tambaram and Avadi
Article Text:
In a major reshuffle in the Tamil Nadu police department, Additional Director General of Police (ADGP) Prem Anand Sinha and ADGP T. Senthilkumar assumed charge as the Commissioners of Police for Tambaram and Avadi respectively on Saturday.

Chennai:





Tambaram and Avadi police commissionerates got new commissioners in a reshuffle of officials on Friday. While additional director-general of police (ADGP) Prem Anand Sinha assumed charge in Tambaram, ADGP T Senthilkumar assumed charge as commissioner of Avadi on Saturday.Prem Anand Sinha succeeds ADGP Sanjaykumar, who had been posted there by the Election Commission ahead of the recent assembly elections in Tamil Nadu. Senthilkumar was with the state administration wing before his appointment as Avadi commissioner.
Article Summary: Prem Anand Sinha succeeds ADGP Sanjaykumar, who had been posted there by the Election Commission ahead of the recent assembly elections in Tamil Nadu. ADGP T Senthilkumar was with the state administration wing before his appointment as Avadi commissioner.

Article Text:
Chennai:





Three men, including a key suspect in a drug peddling case racket, were arrested by the Kannagi Nagar police after a raid at Masjid Nagar on Saturday. Police seized 1.5kg of ganja and 40 narcotic tablets.Based on a tip-off, a police team led by inspector S Saravanan conducted a raid and arrested Vignesh alias Vigna, 30, and Velayudham, 23, both residents of Kannagi Nagar. During interrogation, the accused disclosed that they procured the ganja from the Retteri area in North Chennai and sold it in packets of 10g for ₹500 each.Further inquiry revealed that the gang also sourced narcotic tablets and other contraband from Mumbai for distribution in the city.The team subsequently tracked down an absconding suspect, Premkumar , 24, a key supplier in the network. All the three accused were produced before a magistrate court and remanded in judicial custody.
Article Summary: Police seized 1.5kg of ganja and 40 narcotic tablets from Masjid Nagar. Arrested Vignesh alias Vigna, 30, Velayudham, 23, and Premkumar, 24, a key supplier in the network.

Article Title: Controversy erupts after songs from Vijay movies, TVK campaigns played during convocations at Coimbatore medical colleges
Article Text:
As students approached the stage one by one to receive their degrees, TVK campaign song “Na thannanthani aalu” and TVK viral campaign BGM were played at a very high volume.





COIMBATORE: Some songs from films starring Vijay and a few others associated with the ruling TVK’s election campaigns were played during the convocation ceremonies at the ESI Medical College and the Coimbatore Medical College held on Saturday, sparking a controversy.Ministers K G Arunraj, V Sampath Kumar and K Vignesh, district collector Pavankumar G Giriyappanavar were among those who participated in the convocations.Before the proceedings began, “Raavana Mavanda” and “Oru Pere Varalaru” songs from chief minister C Joseph Vijay’s yet-to-be-released Jana Nayagan movie were played. As students approached the stage one by one to receive their degrees, TVK campaign song “Na thannanthani aalu” and TVK viral campaign BGM were played at a very high volume.As health minister Arunraj was leaving after his address, a TVK party song was played in the background. A few other Vijay movie songs and “Make way for the King” song from Allu Arjun’s Raaka movie, whose edited versions was widely used for Vijay's campaigns, were also played.This sparked a controversy with many netizens stating that there was no discipline at govt events. “This is no govt, and the administration has become a matter of joke,” a social media user remarked.
Article Summary: Some songs from films starring Vijay and a few others associated with the ruling TVK’s election campaigns were played during the convocation ceremonies. This sparked a controversy with many netizens stating that there was no discipline at govt events. “This is no govt, and the administration has become a matter of joke,” a social media user remarked.

Article Title: Sengottaiyan flays previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies on Mekedatu dam issue
Article Text:
K A Sengottaiyan





ERODE: Tamil Nadu revenue minister K A Sengottaiyan on Saturday criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu.Speaking at a consultation meeting of officials from different departments held at the Erode district collectorate, Sengottaiyan reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move to build a dam in Mekedatu.During the review meeting, the minister told officials to deliver results quickly, saying the govt’s performance since coming to power warranted a thorough review and that the people’s demands should be met without delay. “Officers must complete necessary office procedures promptly and ensure citizens receive timely relief,” he told reporters after the meeting.The review, chaired by district collector S Kandasamy and also attended by handloom minister M Vijay Balaji, covered a wide range of administrative concerns.A major focus was the Athikadavu-Avinashi integrated drinking water project. Officials were directed to find out why more than 70 ponds were not getting filled and asked them to take remedial measures. Officials were instructed to begin silt-removal operations in the Bhavanisagar Dam to improve storage capacity and downstream supply.On allegations that liquor outlets were operating in violation of regulations, the revenue minister demanded concrete statistics and warned that strict action would follow where breaches were confirmed.
Article Summary: K A Sengottaiyan criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu. He reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move.
